full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Advent is a really challenging time for me.<|4.60|> <|4.60|> I am particularly challenged with waiting.<|7.28|> <|7.28|> The month of December can feel like torture.<|10.76|> <|10.76|> I'm also very curious.<|13.96|> <|13.96|> Impatience and curiosity are not<|18.12|> <|18.12|> the best combination when I was a little girl.<|21.32|> <|21.32|> Particularly a little girl who was sometimes left home alone.<|26.40|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> A little girl left home alone in December,<|4.70|> <|4.70|> and a little girl who had a mother who was a very bad gift hider.<|10.76|> <|10.76|> You see, I was a practiced,<|14.68|> <|14.68|> successful, and unrepentant gift snooper.<|20.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I wanted only one thing for Christmas in 1975,<|5.24|> <|5.24|> Barbies, pool, and cabana.<|8.60|> <|8.60|> The ads promised it was super deluxe.<|12.38|> <|12.38|> It had a cardboard fence surrounding cardboard bushes,<|16.82|> <|16.82|> and a cardboard field of grass with<|19.98|> <|19.98|> a cardboard barbecue and a plastic pool.<|23.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It was incredible, all simulating the ideal 1970s suburban backyard.<|10.00|> <|10.00|> So you probably have a hint at what's coming.<|14.00|> <|14.00|> I wanted it, and I couldn't wait to find out if I was going to get it for Christmas.<|21.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> So as soon as I was left alone, I searched.<|4.00|> <|4.00|> Remember, I said my mom was not a particularly clever hider.<|9.00|> <|9.00|> It was under the bed in my parents' room.<|12.10|> <|12.10|> And to give my mom credit, it was a really big flat box,<|16.60|> <|16.60|> so her options were limited.<|18.70|> <|18.70|> I could hardly believe it when I found it.<|23.60|> <|23.60|> It was beautiful, just as I'd imagined it.<|28.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> To make a long story short,<|3.88|> <|3.88|> apparently I was not only good at finding clues,<|8.80|> <|8.80|> I was also very good at leaving them behind.<|12.44|> <|12.44|> It took my mom almost no time to discover what I had done.<|18.64|> <|18.64|> I always wonder if it was the tears of joy I shed upon finding it,<|24.48|> <|24.48|> that she discovered on the box and that gave me away.<|28.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Did she yell at me? No.<|4.08|> <|4.08|> Did she spank me? I mean, this was after all the 1970s.<|8.44|> <|8.44|> No. She did tell me how very disappointed she was in me.<|16.36|> <|16.36|> That I had abused her trust and that I had ruined a wonderful surprise.<|23.14|> <|23.14|> Trust me, that was much worse than yelling or spanking.<|27.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> What my mother did next is the stuff of legend.<|6.00|> <|6.00|> It is a story that struck fear into the hearts of our own children when they were told this cautionary tale.<|14.00|> <|14.00|> It was a colossal, life-changing, terrifying, and brilliant parenting move.<|22.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We took it back.<|2.80|> <|2.80|> My mom calmly got the car keys,<|6.10|> <|6.10|> her purse, and the gorgeous and perfect Barbie pool and cabana,<|11.20|> <|11.20|> took me by the hand, and we drove to the Edison Mall.<|15.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We walked into the toy store just days before Christmas, and my mom asked for the manager.<|6.94|> <|8.04|> He came forward and wondered what he could do for us.<|11.24|> <|12.28|> My mom explained that we needed to return the amazing backyard wonderland that was Barbie's Pool and Cabana.<|22.38|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> "No, no, sir, there's nothing wrong with the toy."<|4.70|> <|4.70|> And she then turned to me and asked me to explain why.<|8.58|> <|8.58|> I don't know if your hands are sweating while I'm telling you this, but mine are, just as<|15.54|> <|15.54|> they were that day.<|17.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I confessed my transgression to that manager.<|5.00|> <|5.00|> And he actually looked at my mom like she was the crazy person,<|8.90|> <|8.90|> rather than looking at me like I should be ashamed.<|12.62|> <|12.62|> I chalk that up to the fact that it was a lost sale.<|16.76|> <|16.76|> But I confessed it. It was done.<|21.68|> <|21.68|> I walked out of that toy store with<|25.10|> <|25.10|> more feeling empty than just the shopping bag.<|29.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> To my mother's credit, when we walked away from the toy store, it was over.<|6.50|> <|6.50|> She did not keep reminding me what I had done.<|9.88|> <|9.88|> She did not shame me by telling others of my snooping.<|14.32|> <|14.32|> She could clearly tell that I had learned my lesson and we moved on.<|21.50|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I had a John the Baptist moment with my mom.<|6.50|> <|6.50|> Those are words I'd never imagined saying.<|9.38|> <|9.38|> My beautiful mother with her Texas bred propensity to always look put together.<|16.10|> <|16.10|> Hair and nails done, always the perfect shade of lipstick.<|20.90|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And this wild man, Mark describes, wearing belted camel's hair and eating locust and wild honey while roaming the banks of the Jordan, are nothing alike.<|14.00|> <|14.00|> But their message and their lessons are eerily similar.<|20.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Prepare. Repent. Wait.<|6.00|> <|6.00|> Get yourself ready so that you can enjoy the good stuff that's just around the corner.<|13.00|> <|13.00|> If you're not ready for it, or you rush it, you will miss the full impact.<|19.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> As this morning's collect so succinctly said and prepared us for the readings and the theme of Advent too.<|9.80|> <|11.18|> Merciful God, who sent your messengers, the prophets, to preach repentance and prepare the way for our salvation,<|18.88|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> give us grace to heed their warnings and forsake our sins,<|5.00|> <|6.18|> that we may greet with joy the coming of Jesus Christ,<|9.90|> <|9.90|> our Redeemer.<|10.78|> <|12.76|> What I gained from my Barbie pool and cabana tragicomedy<|17.04|> <|17.94|> was a deeper appreciation for waiting and surprise.<|23.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I also gained a deep sense of relief that come Christmas morning, I wouldn't have to feign wonder.<|7.54|> <|8.92|> You see, this had not been my first foray into gift searching.<|14.20|> <|15.14|> It was just my first time getting caught.<|17.16|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Suddenly, with a clean and repentant heart, Christmas was again about possibility and wonder.<|8.50|> <|8.50|> I felt the weight of the secret snooping fall away.<|13.12|> <|13.12|> I hadn't rushed the season and ruined the surprise.<|16.92|> <|16.92|> I had no guilt.<|18.76|> <|18.76|> I had learned to wait.<|21.80|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> The waiting still isn't easy, but it is so worth it.<|6.94|> <|6.94|> And I can tell you with all honesty, I have never looked for a gift again, ever.<|14.86|> <|14.86|> As Mark reminds us this morning, through the words of Isaiah, "Prepare the way of the<|21.34|> <|21.34|> Lord.<|22.84|> <|22.84|> Make his paths straight."<|25.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Let us prepare and wait for the gift that is Christmas.<|5.78|> <|6.66|> Even in these strange and unsettling days, let us wait with wonder.<|12.44|> <|14.18|> Be well, my friends. I miss you.<|17.88|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.00|><|endoftext|>
